zotero.abstractNote: Algorithmic recourse aims to provide individuals with actionable recommendations to change an unfavorable decision made by a machine learning model. Recent work has shown that recourse can be achieved by leveraging causal models of the data-generating process. However, we show that such causal recourse methods are vulnerable to adversarial attacks: an adversary can manipulate the causal model or the recourse mechanism to provide recourse that is either invalid or harmful to the individual. We formalize this problem and propose a robust framework for causal algorithmic recourse that is resilient to such attacks. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through extensive experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets.

extra.Proceedings Title: Proceedings of the 36th AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence
